segments.text: Say of your brothers,
My people
segments.speaker: Unspecified

segments.text: and of your sisters,
My loved one
segments.speaker: Unspecified

segments.text: Rebuke your mother, rebuke her, for she is not my wife, and I am not her husband. Let her remove the adulterous look from her face and the unfaithfulness from between her breasts.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Otherwise I will strip her naked and make her as bare as on the day she was born; I will make her like a desert, turn her into a parched land, and slay her with thirst.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will not show my love to her children, because they are the children of adultery.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Their mother has been unfaithful and has conceived them in disgrace.
I will go after my lovers, who give me my food and my water, my wool and my linen, my olive oil and my drink.
segments.speaker: Israel (metaphorical wife)

segments.text: Therefore I will block her path with thornbushes; I will wall her in so that she cannot find her way.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: She will chase after her lovers but not catch them; she will look for them but not find them.
I will go back to my husband as at first, for then I was better off than now.
segments.speaker: Israel (metaphorical wife)

segments.text: She has not acknowledged that I was the one who gave her the grain, the new wine and oil, who lavished on her the silver and gold— which they used for Baal.
Therefore I will take away my grain when it ripens, and my new wine when it is ready. I will take back my wool and my linen, intended to cover her naked body.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: So now I will expose her lewdness before the eyes of her lovers; no one will take her out of my hands.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will stop all her celebrations: her yearly festivals, her New Moons, her Sabbath days—all her appointed festivals.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will ruin her vines and her fig trees, which she said were her pay from her lovers; I will make them a thicket, and wild animals will devour them.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will punish her for the days she burned incense to the Baals; she decked herself with rings and jewelry, and went after her lovers, but me she forgot
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: declares the Lord.
Therefore I am now going to allure her; I will lead her into the wilderness and speak tenderly to her.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: There I will give her back her vineyards, and will make the Valley of Achor a door of hope. There she will respond as in the days of her youth, as in the day she came up out of Egypt.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: In that day,
you will call me 'my husband'; you will no longer call me 'my master.'
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: declares the Lord.
I will remove the names of the Baals from her lips; no longer will their names be invoked.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: In that day I will make a covenant for them with the beasts of the field, the birds in the sky and the creatures that move along the ground. Bow and sword and battle I will abolish from the land, so that all may lie down in safety.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will betroth you to me forever; I will betroth you in righteousness and justice, in love and compassion.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will betroth you in faithfulness, and you will acknowledge the Lord.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: In that day,
I will respond
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: declares the Lord—
I will respond to the skies, and they will respond to the earth;
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: and the earth will respond to the grain, the new wine and the olive oil, and they will respond to Jezreel.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will plant her for myself in the land; I will show my love to the one I called 'Not my loved one.' I will say to those called 'Not my people,' 'You are my people'; and they will say, 'You are my God.'
segments.speaker: God
